titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in Japan (Grew) to the Secretary of State

head.hi: The Ambassador in Japan (Grew) to the Secretary of State

div.p: 57. Press correspondents quote Foreign Office spokesman to the effect that the Japanese Legation has been instructed to approach Chang Hsueh-liang in Peiping to induce him to withdraw his forces from the neighborhood of Kupeikow, or to create a neutral zone south of the Wall failing which the Japanese threaten to move into North China in force.

The Military Attaché informs me that the General Staff state that there is some fighting at Kupeikow, where the Great Wall makes a loop; that the Japanese hold the Wall and that there are Chinese troops inside and outside the pass; that the Japanese have insisted that the Chinese withdraw from this area and that it may be necessary for the Japanese to send a detachment down from the Wall on either side of the Chinese force to compel withdrawal; that the Japanese have no further intentions unless the Chinese at the Wall are supported by new troops.
